commands.review_summary:
Adds bundled snapshot plugin CLI metadata plus `openclaw snapshot` create/list/verify/restore commands and SQLite-backed command tests.

PR surface: Source +313, Tests +146. Total +459 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction; this is a feature PR. The command behavior is source-testable from the added tests, but the user-facing packaged CLI path has not been proven in the PR body.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers need to decide the bundled snapshot CLI direction and require real packaged/source CLI proof; there is no narrow automated repair to queue from this review.

target.title: Add snapshot plugin CLI
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds the opt-in `openclaw snapshot` plugin CLI surface.
- Wires `snapshot create`, `snapshot list`, `snapshot verify`, and `snapshot restore` to the local SQLite snapshot provider from PR 1.
- Makes the safe-sync-file workflow user-accessible: create a verified SQLite artifact + manifest that a file-syncing host can persist instead of syncing the live SQLite working set.
- Adds CLI metadata and command alias/activation metadata so the bundled plugin owns the `snapshot` command surface.
- Positions the CLI as the proof surface for #94646's database-first SQLite layout: today it accepts explicit DB paths, and later PRs can map friendly names to global/control-plane and per-agent/data-plane DBs.
- Covers command registration plus create/list/verify/restore command behavior against a real SQLite database.

## Stack

- Depends on #94694
- Database-first state layout: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/94646
- RFC: https://github.com/openclaw/rfcs/pull/20

## Scope

This PR keeps snapshot opt-in and plugin-owned. It does not make every OpenClaw SQLite database automatically durable, does not add object storage, and does not change the runtime database choice.

The point is narrower: give operators and Scout/Lobster-style hosts a command surface that can produce and verify the completed artifact set. Hosts should sync that artifact set, not live `*.sqlite`, `*.sqlite-wal`, `*.sqlite-shm`, or `*.sqlite-journal` churn. File-level deltas over live SQLite remain out of scope; deltas should come later from a verified snapshot cursor.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
The bundled `snapshot` plugin now exposes an opt-in CLI surface for creating, listing, verifying, and restoring SQLite-safe snapshot artifacts. This is the CLI proof layer for the safe sync artifact boundary and the database-first SQLite target model from #94646.

Real environment tested:
Windows source checkout at `C:\src\openclaw-snapshot-pr1`, Node/Vitest source harness, bundled plugin metadata checks, and snapshot command functions backed by a real `node:sqlite` database.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/snapshot/src/cli.test.ts extensions/snapshot/src/snapshot-provider.test.ts`
2. `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugins/bundled-plugin-naming.test.ts src/plugins/bundled-plugin-metadata.test.ts`
3. `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig extensions/snapshot/tsconfig.json extensions/snapshot`
4. `node scripts/check-extension-package-tsc-boundary.mjs --mode=compile`
5. `git diff --check`
6. Previous Codex review: `bash /c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/codex-review/scripts/codex-review --mode local --output .artifacts/snapshot-pr2-codex-review-1.txt`

Evidence after fix:
- Snapshot CLI/provider tests: 2 files passed, 9 tests passed.
- Bundled plugin metadata tests: 2 files passed, 41 tests passed.
- Oxlint completed cleanly for `extensions/snapshot`.
- `git diff --check` completed with no whitespace errors.
- Extension package boundary compile retried after an initial Windows `EBUSY` copyfile lock, then reached the same repo-level package-resolution/type errors seen before this PR: missing `@openclaw/gateway-protocol`, missing `@openclaw/net-policy/*`, and duplicate private `EventStream` declarations through `dist/plugin-sdk`. No snapshot-specific diagnostic was reported before those failures.
- Previous Codex review reported: `codex-review clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.

Observed result after fix:
The `snapshot` plugin registers a CLI command group and the command functions create a snapshot from a real SQLite database, list it, verify manifest/hash/integrity, restore it, and read the restored row successfully.

What was not tested:
Full packaged CLI execution was not tested in this PR; the source harness covers command registration and command behavior directly. Cloud/object-store snapshot repositories, friendly target names for global/per-agent DBs, backup integration, file-sync host integration, SQLite-aware deltas, and failover integration remain follow-up work.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
